title: centaurea glastifolia
intro: Centaurea glastifolia, also known as the Cornflower or knapweed, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Centaurea glastifolia include reducing fever, alleviating menstrual cramps, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in salves and creams for topical application.
